Notice Of Motion And Motion To Preclude Evidence Withheld From Discovery On Fifth Amendment Grounds
Off Calendar Aug-26-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: The Court received and reviewed plaintiff's email re: unavailability to make a court appearance due to medical reasons. Neither party contested the courts tentative ruling

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS' Motion To Preclude Evidence Withheld From Discovery On Fifth Amendment Grounds is denied without prejudice.

Pursuant to CCP 2025.420 [protective order], defendants seek to preclude plaintiff from offering evidence or arguing about certain issues that are germane to this case because plaintiff refused to answer several questions at his deposition based on his Fifth Amendment right against self-incrimination. There is authority for such a motion. (See A&M Records, Inc. v. Heilman (1977) 75 Cal.App.3d 554, 566 ["The accomplishment of this purpose compels the court to prevent a litigant claiming his constitutional privilege against self-incrimination in discovery and then waiving the privilege and testifying at trial. Such a strategy subjects the opposing party to unwarranted surprise. A litigant cannot be permitted to blow hot and cold in this manner."].)

The court, however, denies the motion without prejudice. None of the adduced issues is relevant to disposition of the motion for summary judgment/adjudication presently before the court, which has been denied on procedural grounds. Second, the court agrees with plaintiff that the motion is fairly viewed as a motion in limine that the trial judge should resolve. The trial judge should determine what evidence is or is not allowed based on the specifics before him or her rather than have the trial judge attempt to interpret another judge's ruling. Nothing in this order precludes defendants from refiling the motion should they file a new summary judgment motion, and nothing in this order precludes defendants from objecting to plaintiff's summary judgment evidence in a future summary judgment motion based on plaintiff's invocation of his Fifth Amendment right.

Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. Defendant had substantial justification in bringing the motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; And For Monetary Sanctions [Ccp 2031.310, 2023.030; Crc 2.30]
Off Calendar Aug-14-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, AND THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; And For Monetary Sanctions [Ccp 2031.310, 2023.030; Crc 2.30].

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Kaiser Foundation Hospitals' motion to compel further responses to requests for production, to compel production, and for sanctions is granted in part and denied in part.

On August 1, 2025, after Kaiser filed this motion, plaintiff Doe served amended, verified and objection-free responses as required by the court's November 20, 2024 order. Doe contends his service of the amended responses moots the sanctions request. Kaiser seeks an order that Doe specify which of the 1108 pages he has produced apply to which request for production. Kaiser also presses its request for sanctions.

The court concludes that the volume of discovery produced is not so great that it should make the order Kaiser seeks. The court nonetheless awards sanctions. Sanctions are permitted even where a party responds to discovery requests while the motion is pending. (See Rule of Court 3.1348, subd. (a).) The history recited in the Sha declaration of Kaiser's efforts to obtain code-compliant, verified discovery responses persuades the court that Doe and/or his counsel have misused the discovery process by failing to submit to an authorized method of discovery and making unwarranted objections after a court order that objections were waived. (See Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2023.010, subds. (d), (e), and (g).) It does not appear to the court that Doe would have amended his responses in the absence of this motion. The court finds it appropriate here to award sanctions jointly and severally against plaintiff Doe and his counsel in the amount of $4000. Doe and his counsel shall pay the sanctions within 30 days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Seal Portions Of Plaintiff's Complaint, as follows:

Defendants' "motion to seal portions of plaintiff's complaint" is denied. Defendants argue that the portions are "irrelevant" (Supp. Brf. 2:14), but the court has already ruled to the contrary. (Rply. Brf. 3:6-8.) Further, the complaint (not in any way sealed) has been on the court's public website for 137 days, and defendants knew of this issue even before the complaint was filed. (Opp. 3:1-6.) Thus, a showing of prejudice at this late date is absent. (See CRC 2.550(d)(3).) Whether defendants have standing to assert privacy concerns of third parties or not, it is noteworthy that the third parties have not been heard from.

Order signed after hearing.

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-01-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. EDUARDO G. ROY, Esq. of Prometheus Partners L.L.P. (Tel: 415-527-0255) for plaintiff (via Zoom videoconference);
Atty. ONDREJ LIKAR, Esq. of Buty & Curliano LLP (Tel: 510-267-3000) for defendants Kaiser Foundation Hospitals and the Permanente Medical Group, Inc.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS KASIER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS AND THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' demurrer to the complaint is sustained in part and overruled in part.

1 (CIMA violation): The complaint adequately pleads violation of the California Medical Information Act. (Id. at 8:4-20, 9:4-11:3.) Defendants argue that who improperly received the information must be pled with specificity (Memo. 3:23-24), but the opinions they cite do not so hold. Demurrer overruled.

2 (Civil Code 1798.82 violation): The complaint adequately pleads violation of Civil Code 1798.82. (Id. at 11:4-25.) Defendants argue that when a security-system breach occurred must be pled, but the complaint does that. (Memo. 5:8-9; Cmplt. 8:12-15.) Demurrer overruled.

3 (privacy rights): The complaint adequately alleges invasion of privacy rights. (Id. at 12:1-27.) Demurrer overruled.

4 (negligent misrepresentation, 5 (intentional misrepresentation), 6 (Business & Professions Code 17200): The complaint pleads fraud with insufficient specificity. (Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73 ["how, when, where, to whom, and by what means the representations were tendered"].) Demurrer sustained with leave to amend.

7 (breach of contract): The complaint adequately pleads breach of contract. (Id. at 15:21-16:20; CACI 303.) Demurrer overruled.

8 (breach of fiduciary duty): The complaint adequately pleads breach of fiduciary duty. (Id. at 16:22-17:13.) The case defendants rely on states that medical entities can be secondarily liable for breach of fiduciary duty. (Moore v. Regents of University of California (1990) 51 Cal.3d 120, 133.) Demurrer overruled.

9 (false imprisonment): The complaint adequately pleads false imprisonment. (Id. at 4:26-6:15, 17:15-18:9; CACI 1400.) Demurrer overruled.

10 (assault and battery): The complaint adequately pleads assault and battery. (18:10-19:5.) Demurrer overruled.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
